description: 古都中轴线的起点，百年风云尽在城楼之下

paragraphs.en: Good morning, everyone! Welcome to this amazing journey. I'm your tour guide, Miya, and it's my great honor to be your companion today. We’re now standing at the northern starting point of Tiananmen Square! The bluestones under our feet once saw the imperial processions of the Ming and Qing emperors, and also heard the cheers from the 1949 founding ceremony of the People’s Republic of China. On September 3, a big military parade was held here too—to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the victory of the Chinese People's War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression and the World Anti-Fascist War.
paragraphs.zh: 各位朋友，咱们此刻正站在天安门广场的北侧起点！脚下的青石板曾见证明清皇帝的仪仗，也承载过 1949 年开国大典的欢呼，2025 年9 月 3 日这里还举行了中国人民抗日战争暨世界反法西斯战争胜利 80 周年盛大阅兵。

paragraphs.en: Now, let’s start with our first stop—Tiananmen Gate, right in front of us! Look up, everyone: Tiananmen Gate was first built in 1420, the 18th year of the Yongle reign of the Ming Dynasty. At first, it was called “Chengtianmen” (Gate of Receiving Heavenly Mandate), meaning “getting the mission from heaven to rule,” and it was the main gate of the Forbidden City. In 1651, the 8th year of the Shunzhi reign of the Qing Dynasty, it was rebuilt and renamed “Tiananmen” (Gate of Heavenly Peace), which means “getting the mission from heaven to govern the country peacefully.” Then it was repaired again during the Kangxi reign in 1699—and the gate we see now is almost the same as it was back then. On October 1, 1949, Chairman Mao Zedong announced the founding of the People’s Republic of China here. Since that day, Tiananmen has changed from “a gate for the imperial family only” to “a symbol of the people.”
paragraphs.zh: 大家抬头看，天安门城楼始建于明永乐十八年（1420 年），最初叫 “承天门”，取 “承天启运、受命于天” 之意，是紫禁城的正门；清朝顺治八年（1651 年）改建后改名 “天安门”，寓意 “受命于天、安邦治国”；1699 年康熙年间又经历大修，咱们现在看到的城楼，基本保留了那时的形制。1949 年 10 月 1 日，毛泽东主席在这里宣告中华人民共和国成立，从此，天安门从 “皇家禁地之门” 变成了 “人民的象征”。

paragraphs.en: Now, let’s take a closer look at its architecture—there are many “imperial secrets” here! First, the gate has a “double-eaved gable-and-hip roof” covered with yellow glazed tiles. That yellow color was only used for imperial buildings in ancient times. Then, it has 9 bays (the horizontal sections) and 5 depths (the vertical sections)—this matches the “supremacy of the emperor” in ancient hierarchy. There are 9 rows and 9 columns of door nails (81 in total), 9 lucky beasts on the roof ridge, and even the stone pillars (Huabiao) on both sides are 9.5 meters tall. The number “nine” is everywhere—it’s all about the strict class system in ancient China. Since 1988, when the gate opened to the public, ordinary people like us can also climb up and stand where Chairman Mao once stood to look over the square.
paragraphs.zh: 大家仔细看：城楼是 “重檐歇山顶”，覆盖黄色琉璃瓦 —— 这是皇家建筑的专属配色；面阔 9 间、进深 5 间，暗合 “九五之尊” 的帝王等级；门钉 9 行 9 列共 81 颗，屋脊有 9 只瑞兽，连两侧华表都高 9.5 米。“九” 这个数字贯穿始终，全是古代等级制度的体现。1988 年城楼对公众开放后，咱们普通人也能走上城楼，站在毛主席当年的位置俯瞰广场。

sections.heading_en: 3. Functions in Ming-Qing Dynasties & Modern Significance

paragraphs.en: Back in the Ming and Qing dynasties, Tiananmen was the main place for the imperial family to hold big ceremonies. When the emperor took the throne or had a royal wedding, he would announce orders here. Every year on the Winter Solstice and the 15th day of the first lunar month, he would also give rewards to officials here. But back then, the rules were very strict: ordinary people couldn’t even get close. Only the emperor and empress could walk through the central gate, and princes and ministers had to use the side gates. But since 1988, when the gate opened to the public, we ordinary people can also stand where Chairman Mao announced the founding ceremony and feel how big and grand the square is.
paragraphs.zh: 明清时期，天安门是皇家举行重大仪式的核心场所：皇帝登基、大婚时，会在这里颁布诏令；每年冬至、正月十五，还会在这里赏赐百官。那时候等级森严，普通百姓根本不能靠近，只有皇帝、皇后能走中间的正门，王公大臣走两侧侧门。1988 年城楼正式对公众开放后，普通人也能站在毛主席宣布开国大典的位置，感受广场的壮阔。

paragraphs.en: Now, look at the center of the gate tower—there’s a big portrait of Chairman Mao. It’s 6 meters tall and 4.6 meters wide, and it has been hung here since the 1949 founding ceremony. Every year, people repaint it to keep the colors bright. The background of the portrait is blue sky and white clouds, and below it, there are two red ribbons with words: “Long live the People’s Republic of China” and “Long live the great unity of the people of the world.” This is one of the most obvious symbols of Tiananmen, and it shows the respect of Chinese people for our leader.
paragraphs.zh: 大家看城楼正中央，那幅高 6 米、宽 4.6 米的毛主席画像，从 1949 年开国大典起就悬挂在这里，每年都会重新绘制一次，确保色彩鲜艳。画像背景是蓝天白云，下方有两条红色绶带，写着 “中华人民共和国万岁” 和 “世界人民大团结万岁”—— 这是天安门最鲜明的标志，也是全国人民对领袖的敬意。

sections.heading_zh: 此刻站在金水河旁，手指向桥面）从天安门城楼往下走，咱们脚下这 7 座横跨金水河的汉白玉石桥，就是外金水桥，也是从城楼进入广场的必经之路。
sections.heading_en: II. Outer Golden Water Bridges: Hierarchical Passage from Gate to Square

paragraphs.en: (Now standing by the Golden Water River, pointing to the bridges)After visiting Tiananmen Gate, let’s move down to the next spot—right below the gate tower, there are 7 white marble bridges across the Golden Water River. These are the Outer Golden Water Bridges, and they’re the only way to get from the gate to the square.

You can see these 7 bridges are lined up in a straight line, right next to the main gate of Tiananmen Gate. They divide the Golden Water River into 7 parts. And if you look carefully, the middle bridge is the widest (8.5 meters), and the ones on both sides get narrower one by one. This design isn’t random—it has strict rules about hierarchy.
paragraphs.zh: 7座桥呈 “一字排开”，紧挨着天安门城楼正门，把金水河分成 7 段。大家一眼就能看出，中间的桥最宽（8.5 米），往两边依次变窄 —— 这可不是随便设计的，藏着严格的等级规矩。

sections.heading_zh: 最边上的 2 座叫 “公生桥”：栏杆无复杂雕刻，留给四品以下官员、太监和宫女使用。
sections.heading_en: Let me tell you what each bridge is for:

paragraphs.en: The widest one in the middle is called “Imperial Bridge.” Its railings have dragon carvings—it was the emperor’s “private path,” only he could walk on it. The 2 bridges next to the Imperial Bridge are “Prince Bridges.” Their railings have phoenix carvings, and they were for royal nobles like princes. The next 2 bridges out are “Rank Bridges.” Their railings have lotus carvings, and they were for officials of the third rank and above. The 2 outermost bridges are “Gongsheng Bridges.” Their railings have no fancy carvings—they were for officials below the fourth rank, eunuchs, and maids. Back in the Ming and Qing dynasties, every time the emperor went out of the Forbidden City, guards would line up beside the Imperial Bridge, and officials would stand on their own bridges according to their ranks. It was a very grand scene! Now, these bridges are popular spots for photos—you can even walk on the “Imperial Bridge” to feel what the imperial rules were like.
paragraphs.zh: 明清时期，每次皇帝出紫禁城，御路桥两侧会排列仪仗队，文武百官按品级站在对应桥上，场面特别隆重。现在这些桥成了游客打卡点，大家也能走一走 “御路桥”，感受古代皇家的规制。

paragraphs.en: And if you look closely at the bridges, you’ll see more details. All the bridges are made of white marble. The top of each railing post has lotus petal carvings, and there are stone lions at the bottom of the posts. Every lion has a different pose—some look up at the sky, some bow their heads to touch a ball. The craftsmanship is really fine! After hundreds of years, the white marble is still white and smooth. You can tell how skilled the ancient craftsmen were.
paragraphs.zh: 桥身全用汉白玉打造，栏杆上的望柱顶部雕着莲花瓣，柱底有石狮，每只狮子的姿态都不一样 —— 有的抬头看天，有的低头抚球，工艺特别精细。几百年过去，汉白玉依然洁白光滑，可见古代工匠的手艺有多精湛。

sections.heading_zh: 从外金水桥往广场中央走 300 米左右，咱们就到了第三站——国旗杆。这根高耸的杆子，是整个广场最“庄严的焦点”，每天的升旗仪式都在这里举行。
sections.heading_en: III. National Flagpole: National Symbol in the Center of the Square

paragraphs.en: From the Outer Golden Water Bridges, let’s walk straight toward the center of the square—about 300 meters ahead, we’ll reach our third stop: the national flagpole. This tall pole is the “most solemn spot” in the whole square, and the flag-raising ceremony happens here every day.

This flagpole is 32.6 meters tall—and that height has a meaning. At the 1949 founding ceremony, the original flagpole was 22.5 meters tall. Then in 1991, when they rebuilt it, they changed it to 32.6 meters to match the height of Tiananmen Gate (40.7 meters) visually. The base of the flagpole is a round platform made of white marble. Around it, there are 56 copper posts—they stand for China’s 56 ethnic groups, united closely around our country.
paragraphs.zh: 旗杆高 32.6 米，这个高度有讲究：1949 年开国大典时，旗杆原高 22.5 米；1991 年改建时，为了与天安门城楼（40.7 米）视觉协调，调整为 32.6 米。基座是汉白玉打造的圆形平台，周围有 56 个铜制隔离墩，象征中国 56 个民族团结一心，紧紧围绕国家。

paragraphs.en: Our Five-Star Red Flag was decided at the first plenary session of the Chinese People’s Political Consultative Conference on September 27, 1949. The designer was Zeng Liansong, an ordinary office worker from Shanghai. His design stood out from 2,992 proposals! The red color stands for revolution, and the five yellow stars stand for “the great unity of the people under the leadership of the Communist Party of China.” The big star represents the Party, and the four small stars represent workers, peasants, intellectuals, and the national bourgeoisie. The first Five-Star Red Flag raised (the one used at the 1949 founding ceremony) is now kept in the National Museum of China—it’s a top-level national cultural relic.
paragraphs.zh: 咱们的五星红旗，是 1949 年 9 月 27 日全国政协第一届全体会议确定的。设计者是上海普通职员曾联松，他的方案从 2992 份提案中脱颖而出——红色象征革命，五颗黄星象征“中国共产党领导下的人民大团结”（大星代表党，四颗小星分别代表工人、农民、知识分子和民族资产阶级）。

paragraphs.en: Every morning, when the sun just comes up over the horizon, the honor guard of the People’s Liberation Army marches in neat steps from Tiananmen Gate to the flagpole. When the “March of the Volunteers” starts playing, the national flag rises slowly—and it reaches the top of the pole at the same time as the sun. This ceremony isn’t just a “daily event”; it shows our national dignity and pride. Many tourists get up early on purpose to watch it.
paragraphs.zh: 每天清晨，当太阳刚露出地平线，解放军仪仗队会迈着整齐的正步从天安门城楼走到旗杆下。随着《义勇军进行曲》响起，国旗缓缓上升，与太阳同时达到杆顶。这个仪式不仅是“每天的约定”，更是国家尊严和民族自豪的体现，很多游客特意早起赶来观看。
IV. Monument to the People's Heroes: Timeless Revolutionary Monument）

sections.heading_zh: 四、人民英雄纪念碑：永不褪色的 “革命丰碑”
sections.heading_en: 四、人民英雄纪念碑：永不褪色的 “革命丰碑”

paragraphs.en: (Walking to the monument, slowing down the speech with a solemn tone)Now, let’s keep walking south from the national flagpole. You’ll see a huge gray-white granite building standing there—that’s our fourth stop: the Monument to the People's Heroes. It’s like an “eternal lighthouse,” remembering the heroes who died for China’s revolution since 1840.
paragraphs.zh: （走到纪念碑前，放慢语速，语气肃穆）在国旗杆南侧，咱们看到的这座灰白色花岗岩建筑，就是第四站 —— 人民英雄纪念碑。它像一座 “永恒的灯塔”，纪念着 1840 年以来为中国革命牺牲的英雄们。

paragraphs.en: Building this monument took the hard work of people all over China. On September 30, 1949—the day before the founding ceremony—Chairman Mao personally laid the foundation stone. That stone has the words “Eternal Glory to the People’s Heroes” carved on it. Construction started in August 1952 and finished in May 1958—it took almost 6 years. The most special part is the main stone of the monument. It’s a big granite block: 14.7 meters long, 2.9 meters wide, 1 meter thick, and 60 tons heavy. It was mined from Laoshan Mountain in Qingdao, Shandong. They transported it by train, truck, and even horse-drawn cart—taking 34 days to get to Beijing. Thousands of people helped with the transportation alone!
paragraphs.zh: 纪念碑的建造凝聚了全国人民的心血：1949 年 9 月 30 日，开国大典的前一天，毛主席亲自为纪念碑奠基，埋下了刻有“人民英雄永垂不朽”的基石；1952 年 8 月正式动工，1958 年 5 月建成，前后用了近 6 年时间。
最难得的是碑心石：这块长 14.7 米、宽 2.9 米、厚 1 米、重 60 吨的花岗岩，从山东青岛崂山开采，用火车、汽车、马车接力运输，花了 34 天才运到北京，光运输就动用了数千人。

paragraphs.en: This monument is 37.94 meters tall—about as tall as a 12-story building. It’s the biggest monument in China. The top of the monument has a special roof with four sloping sides and a concave center, and there’s no cover on it. This design is on purpose: it stands for “the heroes’ spirit is eternal, and nothing can hide it.” On the front (north side) of the monument, there are 8 golden characters: “Eternal Glory to the People’s Heroes.” Chairman Mao wrote these words, and the handwriting is strong and powerful. On the back, there’s an inscription written by Premier Zhou Enlai—it has 150 characters, telling the story of China’s revolution from the Opium War to the Liberation War. The last part says: “Going back to 1840, since then, the people’s heroes who died in all struggles to fight internal and external enemies, and to win national independence and people’s freedom and happiness—they are immortal!” These words make people feel deep respect.
paragraphs.zh: 纪念碑总高 37.94米，相当于 12 层楼，是中国最大的纪念碑。碑顶是“盝顶”（四面坡、中间凹的造型），没有覆盖任何屋顶——这是特意设计的，象征“英雄精神永垂不朽，没有任何东西能遮挡”。
碑身正面（北面）刻着毛主席题写的“人民英雄永垂不朽” 8 个鎏金大字，字体刚劲有力；背面刻着周恩来总理题写的碑文，共 150 个字，记录了从鸦片战争到解放战争的革命历程，结尾“由此上溯到一千八百四十年，从那时起，为了反对内外敌人，争取民族独立和人民自由幸福，在历次斗争中牺牲的人民英雄们永垂不朽！”让人肃然起敬。

sections.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

paragraphs.en: And around the base of the monument, there are 8 white marble reliefs. They tell key moments of China’s revolution in order of time. Each relief is 2 meters tall and 2.8 meters wide—together, they’re 40.68 meters long. Let’s list them: Destruction of Opium at Humen (1839, the start of fighting against foreign invasion) Jintian Uprising  (1851, the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom Movement, a major peasant revolution) Wuchang Uprising (1911, the Xinhai Revolution, which ended the feudal monarchy) May 4th Movement (1919, a patriotic movement against imperialism and feudalism) May 30th Movement (1925, an anti-imperialist struggle led by workers) Nanchang Uprising (1927, the start of the Communist Party leading armed struggles on its own) Anti-Japanese Guerrilla War (1931-1945, fighting against Japanese invaders) Victorious Crossing of the Yangtze River (1949, the key battle to liberate all of China) Every relief looks so real! For example, you can see Lin Zexu’s determination in “Destruction of Opium at Humen,” and soldiers charging forward in “Victorious Crossing of the Yangtze River. ” It’s like you can see those passionate years with your own eyes.

sections.heading_zh: （往广场南侧走，指向纪念堂，语气庄重）从人民英雄纪念碑再往南走 50 米，就是第五站 —— 毛主席纪念堂。这座庄严肃穆的建筑，是全国人民缅怀毛主席的地方。
sections.heading_en: V. Mausoleum of Mao Zedong: Spiritual Holy Land to Remember the Great Man

paragraphs.en: (Walking south to the square, pointing to the mausoleum with a solemn tone)Just 50 meters further south from the Monument to the People's Heroes, we reach our fifth stop: the Mausoleum of Mao Zedong. This solemn building is where Chinese people come to remember Chairman Mao.

After Chairman Mao passed away on September 9, 1976, the Party Central Committee decided to build this mausoleum so that people could remember him forever. Construction started on November 24, 1976, and opened on September 9, 1977—the first anniversary of Chairman Mao’s death. It only took 10 months to build, which was called “Chinese speed” in the history of architecture. When building it, 29 provinces, municipalities, and autonomous regions all sent materials: granite from Beijing, white marble from Shandong, glass from Guangdong, steel from Shanghai... Countless workers and craftsmen volunteered to work extra hours, just to finish this “people’s mausoleum” as soon as possible.
paragraphs.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。
建造时，全国 29 个省市自治区都送来材料：北京的花岗岩、山东的汉白玉、广东的玻璃、上海的钢材…… 无数工人、工匠自愿加班，只为早日完成这座 “人民的纪念堂”。

paragraphs.en: This mausoleum is a square building, 33.6 meters tall. Its look mixes traditional and modern styles: it has a yellow glazed tile roof (showing respect for the great man, and using the solemn style of imperial buildings), and 44 granite columns (standing for all Chinese people united closely to build our country). On the top of the door, there are 6 characters: “Mausoleum of Mao Zedong.” Comrade Deng Xiaoping wrote these words in 1977. The handwriting is dignified and grand, making people feel warm and firm.
paragraphs.zh: 纪念堂是正方形建筑，高 33.6 米，外观融合 “传统与现代”：黄色琉璃瓦屋顶（象征对伟人的尊崇，借鉴皇家建筑的庄重感），44 根花岗岩廊柱（象征全国人民紧密团结，共同建设国家）。

sections.heading_zh: 南大厅（出口）：墙上刻着毛主席的诗词《满江红・和郭沫若同志》，“四海翻腾云水怒，五洲震荡风雷激”的句子，展现了毛主席的豪迈气魄。

paragraphs.en: Inside the mausoleum, there are three floors, and the most important part is the “Hall of Respect.” Let’s go through it: North Hall (the entrance): There’s a 3.45-meter-tall white marble sitting statue of Chairman Mao. Behind the statue, there’s a big mural called “The Land of the Motherland.” It has the Great Wall, the Yellow River, and the Yangtze River—showing the beautiful rivers and mountains that Chairman Mao led Chinese people to build. Hall of Respect (the middle floor): Chairman Mao’s body is placed in a crystal coffin. Around the coffin, there are flowers and evergreen trees—they stand for the great man’s spirit being alive forever. Visitors walk slowly with respect to pay tribute to Chairman Mao. South Hall (the exit): On the wall, there’s a poem by Chairman Mao called “Man Jiang Hong · In Reply to Comrade Guo Moruo.” The lines “The four seas churn with clouds and waters angry, the five continents shake with wind and thunder” show Chairman Mao’s heroic spirit.

sections.heading_zh: 南大厅（出口）：墙上刻着毛主席的诗词《满江红・和郭沫若同志》，“四海翻腾云水怒，五洲震荡风雷激”的句子，展现了毛主席的豪迈气魄。
sections.heading_en: VI. Zhengyangmen: Beijing’s Memory at the Southern End of the Square

paragraphs.zh: （走到广场最南端，抬头看正阳门）咱们现在站在天安门广场的最南端，眼前这座红墙灰瓦、有两座塔楼的建筑，就是第六站 —— 正阳门，老北京人都叫它 “前门”，是北京内城的正门。

sections.heading_zh: 六、正阳门：广场南端的 “北京记忆”
sections.heading_en: 六、正阳门：广场南端的 “北京记忆”

paragraphs.en: (Walking to the southernmost end of the square, looking up at Zhengyangmen)Now we’ve walked to the southernmost end of Tiananmen Square. Look up—there’s a building with red walls and gray tiles, and it has two towers. That’s our sixth stop: Zhengyangmen. Old Beijingers call it “Qianmen” (Front Gate), and it was the main gate of Beijing’s inner city in ancient times.
paragraphs.zh: （走到广场最南端，抬头看正阳门）咱们现在站在天安门广场的最南端，眼前这座红墙灰瓦、有两座塔楼的建筑，就是第六站 —— 正阳门，老北京人都叫它 “前门”，是北京内城的正门

paragraphs.en: Zhengyangmen was first built in 1439, the 4th year of the Zhengtong reign of the Ming Dynasty. At first, it was called “Lizhengmen” (Gate of Beautiful Justice), and it was the most magnificent of the 9 gates of Beijing’s inner city back then. In 1644, the first year of the Shunzhi reign of the Qing Dynasty, it was renamed “Zhengyangmen” (Gate of Righteous Sun), which means “upright and facing the sun, national peace and people’s safety.” The ancient people thought that a main gate facing south could “take in yang energy” and bring good luck.
paragraphs.zh: 正阳门始建于明正统四年（1439 年），最初叫 “丽正门”，是明朝北京内城 9 座城门中最气派的一座；清朝顺治元年（1644 年）改名 “正阳门”，取 “正直向阳、国泰民安” 之意 —— 古人认为，正门朝正南，能 “接纳阳气”，带来吉祥。

sections.heading_zh: 正阳门箭楼（南侧的矮楼）：高 35.37米，楼体有 86 个箭窗，是核心防御工事 —— 敌人攻城时，士兵能从箭窗往外射箭；

paragraphs.en: Back in the Ming and Qing dynasties, Zhengyangmen wasn’t just “one building”—it was a group of buildings with three parts: Zhengyangmen Gate Tower (the tall building on the north we see now): It’s 43.65 meters tall. It was the “ceremonial gate” for officials to enter or leave the city.  There were lookouts on the tower, and it also worked for defense. Zhengyangmen Arrow  Tower (the shorter building on the south): It’s 35.37 meters tall, and has 86 arrow windows. It was a key defense building—when enemies attacked the  city, soldiers could shoot arrows from these windows. Barbican (a small semicircular city between the Gate Tower and the Arrow Tower): It’s 198 meters around, with two gates (east and west). If enemies broke into the      barbican, soldiers could attack them from the Gate Tower and Arrow Tower—like “catching a turtle in a jar.” It was very good for defense.
paragraphs.zh: 瓮城（城楼和箭楼之间的半圆形小城）：瓮城周长 198 米，有东西两个门，敌人一旦闯进瓮城，士兵就能从城楼和箭楼上往下攻击，形成 “瓮中捉鳖” 的局面，防御性特别强。

paragraphs.en: In 1900, when the Eight-Power Allied Forces invaded China, the Zhengyangmen Arrow Tower was burned down, and the Gate Tower was also badly damaged. The Qing Dynasty rebuilt the Arrow Tower in 1903 and repaired the Gate Tower in 1916. After the founding of New China, the government has protected and repaired it many times. Now, Zhengyangmen looks almost the same as it did in the Ming and Qing dynasties. It’s like a “city card” of Beijing—many tourists come here to take photos and feel the gate culture of old Beijing.
paragraphs.zh: 1900年八国联军侵华时，正阳门箭楼被烧毁，城楼也受损严重；1903 年清朝重建箭楼，1916 年又修缮了城楼；新中国成立后，政府多次保护修缮，现在的正阳门基本恢复了明清时的模样，成了北京的 “城市名片”，很多游客来这儿拍照片，感受老北京的城门文化。

sections.heading_zh: 七、广场东西两侧：国家的 “政务与文化殿堂”
sections.heading_en: 七、广场东西两侧：国家的 “政务与文化殿堂”

paragraphs.en: Ⅶ. East & West Sides of the Square: National Halls of Government Affairs & Culture (Standing in the center of the square, turning to point to the east and west sides)Now that we’ve visited all the spots along the north-south line of the square, let’s turn to the east and west sides—this is our seventh stop. There are two landmark buildings here: the Great Hall of the People on the west, and the National Museum of China on the east. They’re like “two brothers”—one is for the country’s government affairs, and the other is for culture.
paragraphs.zh: （站在广场中央，转身指向东西两侧）逛完广场南北向的景点，咱们最后看第七站 —— 广场东西两侧的两座标志性建筑：西侧的人民大会堂和东侧的中国国家博物馆，它们像 “一对兄弟”，分别承载着国家的 “政务” 和 “文化” 功能。

paragraphs.en: First, let’s look at the west side—the Great Hall of the People. It’s the “meeting hall” of our country. When it was built: Construction started in October 1958 and finished in September 1959. It only took 10 months! It’s one of the “Ten Great Buildings” built to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the founding of New China. Its size: It’s 206 meters long, 134 meters wide, and 46.5 meters tall. The total building area is 170,000 square meters—about the size of 24 football fields! The roof is light blue, with yellow glazed tile edges. It has a modern look, but also has the charm of traditional Chinese buildings.

sections.heading_zh: 建成时间：1958 年 10 月动工，1959 年 9 月建成，只用了 10 个月，是为庆祝新中国成立 10 周年修建的 “十大建筑” 之一；
sections.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

paragraphs.en: Hall of 10,000 People: It can hold 10,000 people for meetings. The ceiling is light blue, with 500 small lights that look like “stars in the sky.” The National People’s Congress is held here every year. Banquet Hall: It can hold 5,000 people for meals at the same time. State banquets and big receptions are held here—like banquets for foreign leaders. Delegation Halls: All 34 provinces, autonomous regions, municipalities directly under the Central Government, and special administrative regions have their own “delegation halls.” Each has a different decoration style, showing local culture.
paragraphs.zh: 建筑规制：长 206 米，宽 134 米，高 46.5 米，总建筑面积 17 万平方米，相当于 24 个足球场大；屋顶是淡蓝色的，搭配黄色琉璃瓦檐，既有现代感，又有中国传统建筑的韵味；
万人大礼堂（Hall of 10,000 People）：能容纳 1 万人开会，穹顶是淡蓝色的，点缀着 500 盏星灯，像 “满天繁星”，全国人民代表大会每年都在这里召开；

sections.heading_zh: 各省市区代表厅：全国 34 个省、自治区、直辖市、特别行政区都有自己的 “代表厅”，装修风格各有特色，体现地方文化。

paragraphs.en: Now, let’s look at the east side—the National Museum of China. It’s like a “living textbook” of China’s 5,000-year civilization. Its history: Its predecessor was the “National History Museum” set up in 1912. In 1959, it was renamed “Chinese History Museum.” In 2003, it merged with the “Chinese Revolution Museum” to become the “National Museum of China.” Now it’s the biggest comprehensive museum in China, with more than 1.86 million cultural relics.

sections.heading_zh: 2. 东侧：中国国家博物馆——5000年文明的 “活教材”

paragraphs.en: “Ancient China” Exhibition Hall: It shows China’s 5,000-year civilization from the stone tools of primitive society (like those used by Peking Man) to the bronze  wares of the Xia, Shang, and Zhou dynasties (like the national treasure  “Simuwu Ding”), then to the porcelain of the Tang and Song dynasties (like Tang tri-color pottery), and the calligraphy and paintings of the Ming and Qing dynasties. “Road to Rejuvenation” Exhibition Hall: Starting from the Opium War (1840), it tells the story of Chinese people’s struggle—from “being backward and bullied” to “standing up, getting rich, and becoming strong.” There are models of the Destruction of Opium at Humen, flags from the Xinhai Revolution, and videos of the founding ceremony. It’s very educational. Special Exhibitions: It often holds special shows like “Ancient Bronzes,” “Ancient Porcelain,” and “Modern Revolutionary Relics.” Every time, many cultural relic lovers come to visit.
paragraphs.zh: 发展历程：它的前身是 1912 年成立的 “国立历史博物馆”，1959 年改名 “中国历史博物馆”；2003 年，与 “中国革命博物馆” 合并，成立 “中国国家博物馆”，现在是中国最大的综合性博物馆，馆藏文物超过 186 万件；
“古代中国” 展厅：从原始社会的石器（比如北京猿人使用的石器），到夏商周的青铜器（比如国宝 “司母戊鼎”），再到唐宋的瓷器（比如唐三彩）、明清的书画，完整展示了中国 5000 年的文明历程；
“复兴之路” 展厅：从鸦片战争（1840 年）开始，记录中国人民从 “落后挨打” 到 “站起来、富起来、强起来” 的奋斗史，有虎门销烟的模型、辛亥革命的旗帜、开国大典的影像等，特别有教育意义；

paragraphs.en: Alright, everyone—our tour of Tiananmen Square is now over! We started from Tiananmen Gate on the north, walked along the “north→south” route through the Outer Golden Water Bridges, the national flagpole, the Monument to the People's Heroes, the Mausoleum of Mao Zedong, and finally reached Zhengyangmen on the south. We also saw the Great Hall of the People and the National Museum of China on the east and west sides. Every step we took was on a part of history, and every spot has our country’s memories. I hope today’s tour helps you remember the solemnity and warmth of this square. It’s not just a group of buildings with red walls and yellow tiles—it’s a “spiritual landmark” for Chinese people to move from the past to the future. Thank you for joining me today. I wish you a great rest of your trip, and please come back to Tiananmen Square sometime to learn more stories and feel more strength of China!
paragraphs.zh: 各位朋友，天安门广场之旅到这儿就圆满结束啦！咱们从北侧的天安门城楼出发，沿“北→南”路线走过外金水桥、国旗杆、人民英雄纪念碑、毛主席纪念堂，最后到南端的正阳门，还看了东西两侧的人民大会堂和国家博物馆——每一步都踩在历史的印记上，每一处都藏着国家的记忆。
希望今天的讲解，能让大家记住这片广场的庄严与温暖：它不仅是红墙黄瓦的建筑集合，更是中国人民从过去走向未来的 “精神坐标”。感谢大家的陪伴，祝大家接下来的旅程顺利，有空再来天安门广场，感受更多属于中国的故事与力量！

sentences.en: Good morning, everyone! Welcome to this amazing journey.
sentences.zh: 各位朋友，咱们此刻正站在天安门广场的北侧起点！脚下的青石板曾见证明清皇帝的仪仗，也承载过 1949 年开国大典的欢呼，2025 年9 月 3 日这里还举行了中国人民抗日战争暨世界反法西斯战争胜利 80 周年盛大阅兵。

sentences.en: On September 3, a big military parade was held here too—to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the victory of the Chinese People's War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression and the World Anti-Fascist War.

sentences.en: Now, let’s start with our first stop—Tiananmen Gate, right in front of us!
sentences.zh: 大家抬头看，天安门城楼始建于明永乐十八年（1420 年），最初叫 “承天门”，取 “承天启运、受命于天” 之意，是紫禁城的正门；清朝顺治八年（1651 年）改建后改名 “天安门”，寓意 “受命于天、安邦治国”；1699 年康熙年间又经历大修，咱们现在看到的城楼，基本保留了那时的形制。1949 年 10 月 1 日，毛泽东主席在这里宣告中华人民共和国成立，从此，天安门从 “皇家禁地之门” 变成了 “人民的象征”。

sentences.en: Look up, everyone: Tiananmen Gate was first built in 1420, the 18th year of the Yongle reign of the Ming Dynasty.
sentences.zh: 大家抬头看，天安门城楼始建于明永乐十八年（1420 年），最初叫 “承天门”，取 “承天启运、受命于天” 之意，是紫禁城的正门；清朝顺治八年（1651 年）改建后改名 “天安门”，寓意 “受命于天、安邦治国”；1699 年康熙年间又经历大修，咱们现在看到的城楼，基本保留了那时的形制。1949 年 10 月 1 日，毛泽东主席在这里宣告中华人民共和国成立，从此，天安门从 “皇家禁地之门” 变成了 “人民的象征”。

sentences.en: At first, it was called “Chengtianmen” (Gate of Receiving Heavenly Mandate), meaning “getting the mission from heaven to rule,” and it was the main gate of the Forbidden City.
sentences.zh: 大家抬头看，天安门城楼始建于明永乐十八年（1420 年），最初叫 “承天门”，取 “承天启运、受命于天” 之意，是紫禁城的正门；清朝顺治八年（1651 年）改建后改名 “天安门”，寓意 “受命于天、安邦治国”；1699 年康熙年间又经历大修，咱们现在看到的城楼，基本保留了那时的形制。1949 年 10 月 1 日，毛泽东主席在这里宣告中华人民共和国成立，从此，天安门从 “皇家禁地之门” 变成了 “人民的象征”。

sentences.en: In 1651, the 8th year of the Shunzhi reign of the Qing Dynasty, it was rebuilt and renamed “Tiananmen” (Gate of Heavenly Peace), which means “getting the mission from heaven to govern the country peacefully.” Then it was repaired again during the Kangxi reign in 1699—and the gate we see now is almost the same as it was back then.

sentences.en: Since that day, Tiananmen has changed from “a gate for the imperial family only” to “a symbol of the people.”

sentences.en: Now, let’s take a closer look at its architecture—there are many “imperial secrets” here!
sentences.zh: 大家仔细看：城楼是 “重檐歇山顶”，覆盖黄色琉璃瓦 —— 这是皇家建筑的专属配色；面阔 9 间、进深 5 间，暗合 “九五之尊” 的帝王等级；门钉 9 行 9 列共 81 颗，屋脊有 9 只瑞兽，连两侧华表都高 9.5 米。“九” 这个数字贯穿始终，全是古代等级制度的体现。1988 年城楼对公众开放后，咱们普通人也能走上城楼，站在毛主席当年的位置俯瞰广场。

sentences.en: Back in the Ming and Qing dynasties, Tiananmen was the main place for the imperial family to hold big ceremonies.
sentences.zh: 明清时期，天安门是皇家举行重大仪式的核心场所：皇帝登基、大婚时，会在这里颁布诏令；每年冬至、正月十五，还会在这里赏赐百官。那时候等级森严，普通百姓根本不能靠近，只有皇帝、皇后能走中间的正门，王公大臣走两侧侧门。1988 年城楼正式对公众开放后，普通人也能站在毛主席宣布开国大典的位置，感受广场的壮阔。

sentences.en: Now, look at the center of the gate tower—there’s a big portrait of Chairman Mao.

sentences.en: (Now standing by the Golden Water River, pointing to the bridges)After visiting Tiananmen Gate, let’s move down to the next spot—right below the gate tower, there are 7 white marble bridges across the Golden Water River.
sentences.heading_zh: 此刻站在金水河旁，手指向桥面）从天安门城楼往下走，咱们脚下这 7 座横跨金水河的汉白玉石桥，就是外金水桥，也是从城楼进入广场的必经之路。
sentences.heading_en: II. Outer Golden Water Bridges: Hierarchical Passage from Gate to Square

sentences.en: These are the Outer Golden Water Bridges, and they’re the only way to get from the gate to the square.

sentences.heading_en: II. Outer Golden Water Bridges: Hierarchical Passage from Gate to Square

sentences.en: You can see these 7 bridges are lined up in a straight line, right next to the main gate of Tiananmen Gate.
sentences.zh: 7座桥呈 “一字排开”，紧挨着天安门城楼正门，把金水河分成 7 段。大家一眼就能看出，中间的桥最宽（8.5 米），往两边依次变窄 —— 这可不是随便设计的，藏着严格的等级规矩。

sentences.en: They divide the Golden Water River into 7 parts.

sentences.en: The widest one in the middle is called “Imperial Bridge.” Its railings have dragon carvings—it was the emperor’s “private path,” only he could walk on it.

sentences.heading_zh: 最边上的 2 座叫 “公生桥”：栏杆无复杂雕刻，留给四品以下官员、太监和宫女使用。

sentences.en: And if you look closely at the bridges, you’ll see more details.

sentences.en: From the Outer Golden Water Bridges, let’s walk straight toward the center of the square—about 300 meters ahead, we’ll reach our third stop: the national flagpole.
sentences.heading_zh: 从外金水桥往广场中央走 300 米左右，咱们就到了第三站——国旗杆。这根高耸的杆子，是整个广场最“庄严的焦点”，每天的升旗仪式都在这里举行。
sentences.heading_en: III. National Flagpole: National Symbol in the Center of the Square

sentences.en: This tall pole is the “most solemn spot” in the whole square, and the flag-raising ceremony happens here every day.

sentences.heading_en: III. National Flagpole: National Symbol in the Center of the Square

sentences.en: This flagpole is 32.6 meters tall—and that height has a meaning.
sentences.zh: 旗杆高 32.6 米，这个高度有讲究：1949 年开国大典时，旗杆原高 22.5 米；1991 年改建时，为了与天安门城楼（40.7 米）视觉协调，调整为 32.6 米。基座是汉白玉打造的圆形平台，周围有 56 个铜制隔离墩，象征中国 56 个民族团结一心，紧紧围绕国家。

sentences.en: Our Five-Star Red Flag was decided at the first plenary session of the Chinese People’s Political Consultative Conference on September 27, 1949.
sentences.zh: 咱们的五星红旗，是 1949 年 9 月 27 日全国政协第一届全体会议确定的。设计者是上海普通职员曾联松，他的方案从 2992 份提案中脱颖而出——红色象征革命，五颗黄星象征“中国共产党领导下的人民大团结”（大星代表党，四颗小星分别代表工人、农民、知识分子和民族资产阶级）。

sentences.en: Every morning, when the sun just comes up over the horizon, the honor guard of the People’s Liberation Army marches in neat steps from Tiananmen Gate to the flagpole.
sentences.zh: 每天清晨，当太阳刚露出地平线，解放军仪仗队会迈着整齐的正步从天安门城楼走到旗杆下。随着《义勇军进行曲》响起，国旗缓缓上升，与太阳同时达到杆顶。这个仪式不仅是“每天的约定”，更是国家尊严和民族自豪的体现，很多游客特意早起赶来观看。

sentences.en: When the “March of the Volunteers” starts playing, the national flag rises slowly—and it reaches the top of the pole at the same time as the sun.

sentences.en: (Walking to the monument, slowing down the speech with a solemn tone)Now, let’s keep walking south from the national flagpole.

sentences.en: Building this monument took the hard work of people all over China.
sentences.zh: 纪念碑的建造凝聚了全国人民的心血：1949 年 9 月 30 日，开国大典的前一天，毛主席亲自为纪念碑奠基，埋下了刻有“人民英雄永垂不朽”的基石；1952 年 8 月正式动工，1958 年 5 月建成，前后用了近 6 年时间。

sentences.en: They transported it by train, truck, and even horse-drawn cart—taking 34 days to get to Beijing.

sentences.en: This monument is 37.94 meters tall—about as tall as a 12-story building.
sentences.zh: 纪念碑总高 37.94米，相当于 12 层楼，是中国最大的纪念碑。碑顶是“盝顶”（四面坡、中间凹的造型），没有覆盖任何屋顶——这是特意设计的，象征“英雄精神永垂不朽，没有任何东西能遮挡”。

sentences.en: On the back, there’s an inscription written by Premier Zhou Enlai—it has 150 characters, telling the story of China’s revolution from the Opium War to the Liberation War.

sentences.en: The last part says: “Going back to 1840, since then, the people’s heroes who died in all struggles to fight internal and external enemies, and to win national independence and people’s freedom and happiness—they are immortal!” These words make people feel deep respect.

sentences.en: And around the base of the monument, there are 8 white marble reliefs.
sentences.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

sentences.en: They tell key moments of China’s revolution in order of time.
sentences.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

sentences.en: Each relief is 2 meters tall and 2.8 meters wide—together, they’re 40.68 meters long.
sentences.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

sentences.en: Let’s list them: Destruction of Opium at Humen (1839, the start of fighting against foreign invasion) Jintian Uprising  (1851, the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom Movement, a major peasant revolution) Wuchang Uprising (1911, the Xinhai Revolution, which ended the feudal monarchy) May 4th Movement (1919, a patriotic movement against imperialism and feudalism) May 30th Movement (1925, an anti-imperialist struggle led by workers) Nanchang Uprising (1927, the start of the Communist Party leading armed struggles on its own) Anti-Japanese Guerrilla War (1931-1945, fighting against Japanese invaders) Victorious Crossing of the Yangtze River (1949, the key battle to liberate all of China) Every relief looks so real!
sentences.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

sentences.en: For example, you can see Lin Zexu’s determination in “Destruction of Opium at Humen,” and soldiers charging forward in “Victorious Crossing of the Yangtze River. ” It’s like you can see those passionate years with your own eyes.
sentences.heading_zh: 每一块浮雕都栩栩如生，比如 “虎门销烟” 里林则徐的坚定、“胜利渡长江” 里战士的冲锋，仿佛能让人看到当年的热血岁月。

sentences.en: (Walking south to the square, pointing to the mausoleum with a solemn tone)Just 50 meters further south from the Monument to the People's Heroes, we reach our fifth stop: the Mausoleum of Mao Zedong.
sentences.heading_zh: （往广场南侧走，指向纪念堂，语气庄重）从人民英雄纪念碑再往南走 50 米，就是第五站 —— 毛主席纪念堂。这座庄严肃穆的建筑，是全国人民缅怀毛主席的地方。
sentences.heading_en: V. Mausoleum of Mao Zedong: Spiritual Holy Land to Remember the Great Man

sentences.en: This solemn building is where Chinese people come to remember Chairman Mao.
sentences.heading_zh: （往广场南侧走，指向纪念堂，语气庄重）从人民英雄纪念碑再往南走 50 米，就是第五站 —— 毛主席纪念堂。这座庄严肃穆的建筑，是全国人民缅怀毛主席的地方。
sentences.heading_en: V. Mausoleum of Mao Zedong: Spiritual Holy Land to Remember the Great Man

sentences.en: After Chairman Mao passed away on September 9, 1976, the Party Central Committee decided to build this mausoleum so that people could remember him forever.
sentences.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。

sentences.en: Construction started on November 24, 1976, and opened on September 9, 1977—the first anniversary of Chairman Mao’s death.
sentences.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。

sentences.en: It only took 10 months to build, which was called “Chinese speed” in the history of architecture.
sentences.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。

sentences.en: When building it, 29 provinces, municipalities, and autonomous regions all sent materials: granite from Beijing, white marble from Shandong, glass from Guangdong, steel from Shanghai...
sentences.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。

sentences.en: Countless workers and craftsmen volunteered to work extra hours, just to finish this “people’s mausoleum” as soon as possible.
sentences.zh: 1976年 9 月 9 日毛主席逝世后，为了让人民能永远缅怀他，党中央决定修建纪念堂。1976 年 11 月 24 日动工，1977 年 9 月 9 日（毛主席逝世一周年）建成开放，只用了 10 个月时间，创造了建筑史上的 “中国速度”。

sentences.en: This mausoleum is a square building, 33.6 meters tall.
sentences.zh: 纪念堂是正方形建筑，高 33.6 米，外观融合 “传统与现代”：黄色琉璃瓦屋顶（象征对伟人的尊崇，借鉴皇家建筑的庄重感），44 根花岗岩廊柱（象征全国人民紧密团结，共同建设国家）。

sentences.en: Its look mixes traditional and modern styles:

sentences.en: Inside the mausoleum, there are three floors, and the most important part is the “Hall of Respect.” Let’s go through it: North Hall (the entrance): There’s a 3.45-meter-tall white marble sitting statue of Chairman Mao.
sentences.heading_zh: 南大厅（出口）：墙上刻着毛主席的诗词《满江红・和郭沫若同志》，“四海翻腾云水怒，五洲震荡风雷激”的句子，展现了毛主席的豪迈气魄。

sentences.en: Behind the statue, there’s a big mural called “The Land of the Motherland.” It has the Great Wall, the Yellow River, and the Yangtze River—showing the beautiful rivers and mountains that Chairman Mao led Chinese people to build.
sentences.heading_zh: 南大厅（出口）：墙上刻着毛主席的诗词《满江红・和郭沫若同志》，“四海翻腾云水怒，五洲震荡风雷激”的句子，展现了毛主席的豪迈气魄。

sentences.en: Hall of Respect (the middle floor): Chairman Mao’s body is placed in a crystal coffin.

sentences.en: Around the coffin, there are flowers and evergreen trees—they stand for the great man’s spirit being alive forever.

sentences.en: South Hall (the exit): On the wall, there’s a poem by Chairman Mao called “Man Jiang Hong · In Reply to Comrade Guo Moruo.” The lines “The four seas churn with clouds and waters angry, the five continents shake with wind and thunder” show Chairman Mao’s heroic spirit.

sentences.en: Zhengyangmen was first built in 1439, the 4th year of the Zhengtong reign of the Ming Dynasty.
sentences.zh: 正阳门始建于明正统四年（1439 年），最初叫 “丽正门”，是明朝北京内城 9 座城门中最气派的一座；清朝顺治元年（1644 年）改名 “正阳门”，取 “正直向阳、国泰民安” 之意 —— 古人认为，正门朝正南，能 “接纳阳气”，带来吉祥。

sentences.en: At first, it was called “Lizhengmen” (Gate of Beautiful Justice), and it was the most magnificent of the 9 gates of Beijing’s inner city back then.
sentences.zh: 正阳门始建于明正统四年（1439 年），最初叫 “丽正门”，是明朝北京内城 9 座城门中最气派的一座；清朝顺治元年（1644 年）改名 “正阳门”，取 “正直向阳、国泰民安” 之意 —— 古人认为，正门朝正南，能 “接纳阳气”，带来吉祥。

sentences.en: In 1644, the first year of the Shunzhi reign of the Qing Dynasty, it was renamed “Zhengyangmen” (Gate of Righteous Sun), which means “upright and facing the sun, national peace and people’s safety.” The ancient people thought that a main gate facing south could “take in yang energy” and bring good luck.
sentences.zh: 正阳门始建于明正统四年（1439 年），最初叫 “丽正门”，是明朝北京内城 9 座城门中最气派的一座；清朝顺治元年（1644 年）改名 “正阳门”，取 “正直向阳、国泰民安” 之意 —— 古人认为，正门朝正南，能 “接纳阳气”，带来吉祥。

sentences.en: Back in the Ming and Qing dynasties, Zhengyangmen wasn’t just “one building”—it was a group of buildings with three parts: Zhengyangmen Gate Tower (the tall building on the north we see now): It’s 43.65 meters tall.
sentences.zh: 瓮城（城楼和箭楼之间的半圆形小城）：瓮城周长 198 米，有东西两个门，敌人一旦闯进瓮城，士兵就能从城楼和箭楼上往下攻击，形成 “瓮中捉鳖” 的局面，防御性特别强。
sentences.heading_zh: 正阳门箭楼（南侧的矮楼）：高 35.37米，楼体有 86 个箭窗，是核心防御工事 —— 敌人攻城时，士兵能从箭窗往外射箭；

sentences.en: It was the “ceremonial gate” for officials to enter or leave the city.

sentences.en: In 1900, when the Eight-Power Allied Forces invaded China, the Zhengyangmen Arrow Tower was burned down, and the Gate Tower was also badly damaged.
sentences.zh: 1900年八国联军侵华时，正阳门箭楼被烧毁，城楼也受损严重；1903 年清朝重建箭楼，1916 年又修缮了城楼；新中国成立后，政府多次保护修缮，现在的正阳门基本恢复了明清时的模样，成了北京的 “城市名片”，很多游客来这儿拍照片，感受老北京的城门文化。

sentences.en: The Qing Dynasty rebuilt the Arrow Tower in 1903 and repaired the Gate Tower in 1916.

sentences.en: Ⅶ. East & West Sides of the Square:
sentences.zh: （站在广场中央，转身指向东西两侧）逛完广场南北向的景点，咱们最后看第七站 —— 广场东西两侧的两座标志性建筑：西侧的人民大会堂和东侧的中国国家博物馆，它们像 “一对兄弟”，分别承载着国家的 “政务” 和 “文化” 功能。

sentences.en: First, let’s look at the west side—the Great Hall of the People.
It’s the “meeting hall” of our country.
When it was built: Construction started in October 1958 and finished in September 1959.
It only took 10 months! It’s one of the “Ten Great Buildings” built to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the founding of New China.
Its size: It’s 206 meters long, 134 meters wide, and 46.5 meters tall.
The total building area is 170,000 square meters—about the size of 24 football fields!
The roof is light blue, with yellow glazed tile edges.
It has a modern look, but also has the charm of traditional Chinese buildings.
Hall of 10,000 People: It can hold 10,000 people for meetings.
sentences.zh: 建筑规制：长 206 米，宽 134 米，高 46.5 米，总建筑面积 17 万平方米，相当于 24 个足球场大；屋顶是淡蓝色的，搭配黄色琉璃瓦檐，既有现代感，又有中国传统建筑的韵味；
sentences.heading_zh: 建成时间：1958 年 10 月动工，1959 年 9 月建成，只用了 10 个月，是为庆祝新中国成立 10 周年修建的 “十大建筑” 之一；
sentences.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

sentences.en: The ceiling is light blue, with 500 small lights that look like “stars in the sky.” The National People’s Congress is held here every year.
sentences.zh: 建筑规制：长 206 米，宽 134 米，高 46.5 米，总建筑面积 17 万平方米，相当于 24 个足球场大；屋顶是淡蓝色的，搭配黄色琉璃瓦檐，既有现代感，又有中国传统建筑的韵味；

sentences.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

sentences.en: Banquet Hall: It can hold 5,000 people for meals at the same time.
sentences.zh: 建筑规制：长 206 米，宽 134 米，高 46.5 米，总建筑面积 17 万平方米，相当于 24 个足球场大；屋顶是淡蓝色的，搭配黄色琉璃瓦檐，既有现代感，又有中国传统建筑的韵味；
sentences.heading_zh: 建成时间：1958 年 10 月动工，1959 年 9 月建成，只用了 10 个月，是为庆祝新中国成立 10 周年修建的 “十大建筑” 之一；
sentences.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

sentences.en: State banquets and big receptions are held here—like banquets for foreign leaders.

sentences.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

sentences.en: Delegation Halls: All 34 provinces, autonomous regions, municipalities directly under the Central Government, and special administrative regions have their own “delegation halls.” Each has a different decoration style, showing local culture.

sentences.heading_en: What’s inside and what it’s used for:

sentences.en: Now, let’s look at the east side—the National Museum of China.
sentences.heading_zh: 各省市区代表厅：全国 34 个省、自治区、直辖市、特别行政区都有自己的 “代表厅”，装修风格各有特色，体现地方文化。

sentences.en: It’s like a “living textbook” of China’s 5,000-year civilization.
sentences.heading_zh: 各省市区代表厅：全国 34 个省、自治区、直辖市、特别行政区都有自己的 “代表厅”，装修风格各有特色，体现地方文化。

sentences.en: Its history: Its predecessor was the “National History Museum” set up in 1912.
sentences.heading_zh: 各省市区代表厅：全国 34 个省、自治区、直辖市、特别行政区都有自己的 “代表厅”，装修风格各有特色，体现地方文化。

sentences.en: In 1959, it was renamed “Chinese History Museum.” In 2003, it merged with the “Chinese Revolution Museum” to become the “National Museum of China.” Now it’s the biggest comprehensive museum in China, with more than 1.86 million cultural relics.
sentences.heading_zh: 各省市区代表厅：全国 34 个省、自治区、直辖市、特别行政区都有自己的 “代表厅”，装修风格各有特色，体现地方文化。

sentences.en: “Ancient China” Exhibition Hall: It shows China’s 5,000-year civilization from the stone tools of primitive society (like those used by Peking Man) to the bronze  wares of the Xia, Shang, and Zhou dynasties (like the national treasure  “Simuwu Ding”), then to the porcelain of the Tang and Song dynasties (like Tang tri-color pottery), and the calligraphy and paintings of the Ming and Qing dynasties. “Road to Rejuvenation” Exhibition Hall:
sentences.zh: 发展历程：它的前身是 1912 年成立的 “国立历史博物馆”，1959 年改名 “中国历史博物馆”；2003 年，与 “中国革命博物馆” 合并，成立 “中国国家博物馆”，现在是中国最大的综合性博物馆，馆藏文物超过 186 万件；
sentences.heading_zh: 2. 东侧：中国国家博物馆——5000年文明的 “活教材”

sentences.en: Starting from the Opium War (1840), it tells the story of Chinese people’s struggle—from “being backward and bullied” to “standing up, getting rich, and becoming strong.” There are models of the Destruction of Opium at Humen, flags from the Xinhai Revolution, and videos of the founding ceremony.
sentences.zh: 发展历程：它的前身是 1912 年成立的 “国立历史博物馆”，1959 年改名 “中国历史博物馆”；2003 年，与 “中国革命博物馆” 合并，成立 “中国国家博物馆”，现在是中国最大的综合性博物馆，馆藏文物超过 186 万件；
sentences.heading_zh: 2. 东侧：中国国家博物馆——5000年文明的 “活教材”

sentences.en: It’s very educational. Special Exhibitions: It often holds special shows like “Ancient Bronzes,” “Ancient Porcelain,” and “Modern Revolutionary Relics.” Every time, many cultural relic lovers come to visit.
sentences.zh: 发展历程：它的前身是 1912 年成立的 “国立历史博物馆”，1959 年改名 “中国历史博物馆”；2003 年，与 “中国革命博物馆” 合并，成立 “中国国家博物馆”，现在是中国最大的综合性博物馆，馆藏文物超过 186 万件；
sentences.heading_zh: 2. 东侧：中国国家博物馆——5000年文明的 “活教材”

sentences.en: Alright, everyone—our tour of Tiananmen Square is now over!
sentences.zh: 各位朋友，天安门广场之旅到这儿就圆满结束啦！咱们从北侧的天安门城楼出发，沿“北→南”路线走过外金水桥、国旗杆、人民英雄纪念碑、毛主席纪念堂，最后到南端的正阳门，还看了东西两侧的人民大会堂和国家博物馆——每一步都踩在历史的印记上，每一处都藏着国家的记忆。

sentences.en: We started from Tiananmen Gate on the north, walked along the “north→south” route through the Outer Golden Water Bridges, the national flagpole, the Monument to the People's Heroes, the Mausoleum of Mao Zedong, and finally reached Zhengyangmen on the south.
